title: Run the full MaxDiff wrangling pipeline end-to-end
summary: Run the full MaxDiff data-wrangling pipeline in one step: prepare raw survey data, build choice sets, and produce an estimation-ready coded-long-format CSV. Equivalent to running `prepare-maxdiff`, `build-choice-sets`, and `build-estimation-long` in sequence.
description: Convenience wrapper that runs all three stages of the MaxDiff wrangling pipeline (`prepare-maxdiff` → `build-choice-sets` → `build-estimation-long`) in a single call, with a single shared output directory. Designed as the recommended entry point for users who don't need to inspect intermediate files. The argument surface is a superset of `prepare-maxdiff`'s — the same column-name overrides apply, and all intermediate stages are wired together internally.
usage:
[["choice-wrangling::pipeline-maxdiff"]]
design_csv = "<design.csv>"
respondent_csv = "<respondents.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "pilot"
# optional column-name overrides (same as prepare-maxdiff):
prefix = "MaxDiff"
respondent_id = "sys_RespNum"
version_col = "sys_MXDVersion_MaxDiff"
best_columns = ["..."]
worst_columns = ["..."]
task_numbers = [1, 2, 3]

arguments.name: design_csv
arguments.description: Path to the experimental design CSV (wide format). Same shape as `prepare-maxdiff` expects: one row per (version, task) with columns identifying the version, task number, and the items shown in each slot of that task.

arguments.name: respondent_csv
arguments.description: Path to the wide-format respondent survey CSV. Must contain a respondent ID, a design-version column, and one column per (task, slot) for the best / worst selections. Auto-discovery handles the typical Decipher naming convention; use the explicit `best_columns` / `worst_columns` overrides for non-Decipher exports.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for all pipeline files. Created if it does not exist. Receives the prepared respondent CSV, the choice-sets CSV, the task-metadata CSV, the coded long-format CSV, and the cookbook JSON — i.e., the union of all three stages' outputs.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Non-empty string. Used to name output artifacts and subdirectories so multiple runs under the same run_dir do not collide. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: prefix
arguments.description: Column-name prefix used to auto-discover best / worst selection columns when explicit `best_columns` / `worst_columns` are not supplied. With the default, the loader looks for columns matching `<prefix>_b<n>` and `<prefix>_w<n>`.

arguments.name: respondent_id
arguments.description: Name of the respondent ID column in `respondent_csv`. Defaults to Decipher's system column naming. Override for any non-Decipher export.

arguments.name: version_col
arguments.description: Name of the design-version column in `respondent_csv` — the column that records which version of the design each respondent saw. Used to join respondent selections against the right rows of `design_csv`.

arguments.name: selection_value_type
arguments.description: How best / worst selection cells encode the chosen item. `"item_name"` (default) means cells contain the item label directly.

arguments.name: best_columns
arguments.description: Explicit list of best-selection columns in `respondent_csv`, in task order. Use to bypass auto-discovery for non-Decipher exports.

arguments.name: worst_columns
arguments.description: Explicit list of worst-selection columns in `respondent_csv`, in task order. Optional in best-only studies.

arguments.name: task_numbers
arguments.description: Explicit list of task numbers (1-based) corresponding to `best_columns` / `worst_columns`. Use when task numbers are non-contiguous or do not start at 1.

arguments.name: design_version_col
arguments.description: Name of the design-version column in `design_csv`. Defaults to Decipher-style auto-discovery.

arguments.name: design_task_col
arguments.description: Name of the task-number column in `design_csv`. Defaults to Decipher-style auto-discovery.

arguments.name: design_item_cols
arguments.description: Explicit list of item-slot columns in `design_csv`, in slot order.

arguments.name: include_original_columns
arguments.description: When `true`, the prepared respondent CSV stage retains the original respondent-side selection columns alongside the normalised ones. Default `false` for the pipeline wrapper to keep intermediate files lean.

arguments.name: negate_worst_utilities
arguments.description: When `true` (the default for the pipeline wrapper), the final `build-estimation-long` stage negates feature columns for worst-side rows so a single MNL fit can absorb both halves of the best/worst response. This matches what the bundled `model-mnl-lg`, `model-mnl-lc`, and `model-mnl-hb-mh` plugins expect. Set to `false` only if your downstream model handles best / worst separately.

arguments.name: alternative_position_name
arguments.description: Name of the alternative-position column to write in the coded-long output (1-based slot index). Required if you want position-effect terms in the model. Omit to skip position columns entirely.

arguments.name: add_best_position_attribute
arguments.description: When `true`, add a coded position attribute for best-side responses. Effective only if `alternative_position_name` is also set.

arguments.name: add_worst_position_attribute
arguments.description: When `true`, add a coded position attribute for worst-side responses, capturing slot-position effects on worst picks separately from best picks.

details:
`pipeline-maxdiff` is the recommended one-shot entry point into the wrangling pipeline. It internally invokes `prepare-maxdiff`, `build-choice-sets`, and `build-estimation-long` against a shared output directory, threading the right column names and intermediate file paths between stages so the user doesn't have to.

The argument surface is a superset of `prepare-maxdiff`'s, with three additions from the estimation-long stage: `negate_worst_utilities`, `alternative_position_name`, and the two `add_*_position_attribute` toggles. The defaults are tuned for the common case: best-worst data, Decipher conventions on the respondent side, and downstream consumption by one of the bundled modelling plugins (so `negate_worst_utilities` defaults to `true` here, unlike the standalone `build-estimation-long` command).

Use the three-stage form (`prepare-maxdiff` → `build-choice-sets` → `build-estimation-long`) instead of this wrapper when you need to inspect intermediate files, re-run a single stage with different settings, or apply a non-default coding spec via `coding_json`. The pipeline wrapper deliberately does not expose `coding_json` — it builds the estimation file with the default per-attribute coding decisions.
value:
On success, `out_dir` contains the union of all three stages' outputs:

- The prepared respondent CSV (from `prepare-maxdiff`).
- The choice-sets CSV and task-metadata CSV (from `build-choice-sets`).
- The coded long-format CSV and cookbook JSON (from `build-estimation-long`).

The coded long CSV plus cookbook are what downstream estimation plugins (`model-mnl-lg`, `model-mnl-lc`, `model-mnl-hb-mh`) consume directly.

In protocol mode the plugin emits `event.run.started`, then `event.run.finished` (or `event.run.failed`).
notes: If you need to apply a non-default coding spec (effects vs dummy vs interp_effects per attribute), use the three-stage form and supply `coding_json` to `build-estimation-long`. The pipeline wrapper uses default coding throughout.
`negate_worst_utilities` defaults to `true` here (vs `false` in the standalone `build-estimation-long` command) because the pipeline assumes you're feeding the bundled best-worst MNL plugins.
Intermediate files are not deleted at the end of the run — you can inspect or reuse them after the fact.

see_also.description: First stage of the pipeline, run standalone.
Second stage of the pipeline, run standalone.
Third stage of the pipeline, run standalone — needed if you want to supply a custom coding spec.
Inverse operation that uses the cookbook JSON produced by this pipeline.
Generate the experimental design CSV that this pipeline consumes.

examples.title: End-to-end run on a Qualtrics export
examples.body:
[["choice-wrangling::pipeline-maxdiff"]]
design_csv = "design.csv"
respondent_csv = "qualtrics_export.csv"
run_dir = "./wrangled"
run_name = "pilot2"
respondent_id = "ResponseId"
version_col = "design_version"
best_columns = ["Q1_best", "Q2_best", "Q3_best", "Q4_best"]
worst_columns = ["Q1_worst", "Q2_worst", "Q3_worst", "Q4_worst"]
task_numbers = [1, 2, 3, 4]

examples.title: End-to-end run with separate best / worst position effects
examples.body:
[["choice-wrangling::pipeline-maxdiff"]]
design_csv = "design.csv"
respondent_csv = "respondents.csv"
run_dir = "./wrangled"
run_name = "pilot3"
alternative_position_name = "alternative_position"
add_best_position_attribute = true
add_worst_position_attribute = true
